title: 中考六选五 17: Why Small Habits Matter
passage:
Big goals are exciting. Students may decide to improve their English, become healthier or read more books. 1. ______ Without daily habits, even a good goal can remain only a wish.

Small habits are powerful because they are easier to repeat. Reading five pages a day may not sound impressive. 2. ______ After a few months, however, the result can become meaningful.

Another advantage is that small habits reduce pressure. If students set a goal that is too large, they may give up quickly. 3. ______ A small step feels possible, so they are more likely to continue.

Small habits also shape identity. A student who writes two English sentences every day may slowly begin to see himself as someone who practises English regularly. 4. ______ This change in identity can make the habit stronger.

Of course, small habits still need direction. Doing something small every day is not enough if it does not lead anywhere. 5. ______ The best habits are small enough to continue but clear enough to support a larger goal.

questions.label: Questions 1-5 - Sentence Insertion
questions.instruction: Choose the best sentence from A-F for each blank in the passage. There is one extra sentence you do not need.

choices.text: But goals alone do not create change.
It may even feel too simple to be useful.
This is especially true when progress is not quick or obvious.
They should be connected to a clear purpose.
Over time, behaviour can influence how a person sees himself.
Students should only focus on big actions because small actions are too slow.
